_doc: Verbatim, scrubbed responses captured from the original uipath-troubleshoot session that diagnosed a Faulted GSuite Create Folder job in folder Shared. Job/folder keys, the conflicting name `dupe-folder`, and all exception text are preserved exactly; hostname, email, and Windows account were scrubbed (UIP-PW06WJSK -> MOCK-HOST, dan.morosanu@uipath.com -> original_email@test.com, UIPATH\DAN.MOROSANU -> UIPATH\REPLACEMENT_USER). First-match-wins: specific patterns precede generic ones. `is connections list` is a DEFENSIVE fixture proving both GSuite connections serving Shared are valid/Enabled (the fault is NOT a connection problem). `or jobs traces` returns the real non-JSON Failure envelope the live CLI produced.

unmocked_default._doc: Any command with no matching rule returns an empty JSON array with exit 0, mirroring an empty list result. Keeps the agent from hard-failing on an unrecorded probe.

expected_calls.description: Agent lists folders to resolve folder Shared to its key (1965a46b-...).
Agent finds the most-recent Faulted job in Shared (6c8b1949 is the latest by StartTime).
Agent reads the faulted job's Info, exposing the GSuiteException name-conflict (`Multiple items with the name dupe-folder found in the specified folder.`) thrown from CreateFolderConnections.
OPTIONAL -- Agent may list connections to rule out a connection/auth problem; the fixture shows both GSuite connections Enabled. Tracked-but-not-required.
OPTIONAL -- traces are unavailable for this job (non-JSON Failure envelope); tracked-but-not-required.
